claim: The `CH.openSettings` key name deviates from the `domainVerb` naming convention used by every other push-channel key in the CH map.
evidence: All eleven other main->renderer push-channel keys follow a domain-prefix pattern: `appFlush`, `winAssignment`, `termSerialize`, `ptyData`, `ptyStatus`, `gitStatus`, `cloudStatus`, `aiSession`, `usageMetrics`, `recState`, `envState`. The new key is `openSettings` with no domain prefix; by analogy it should be `menuOpenSettings`. The string value 'menu:open-settings' is correctly formatted (domain:verb per CLAUDE.md), but the TS key name is inconsistent with its neighbors.
recommendation: Rename the CH key to `menuOpenSettings` and update every reference (src/main/menu.ts:20, src/preload/index.ts:103, src/renderer/App.tsx:57) to CH.menuOpenSettings. Pure rename with no runtime impact.

claim: A UUID is allocated unconditionally before the suppression guard, so every suppressed pushToast call creates and immediately discards a UUID.
evidence: `const id = uuid()` on line 18, then `if (get().quick.toastsEnabled !== true) return id` on line 19. The inline comment explains the intent ('string-id return contract is preserved so no call site needs to special-case the disabled state'), making this a deliberate spec decision (REQ-006). Practical cost is negligible, but the ordering is the reverse of what a future reader might expect (generate only when needed).
recommendation: No action required; the design is intentional and spec-sanctioned. Consider strengthening the inline comment to explicitly state the UUID is generated before the guard by design, not by accident, to prevent a future maintainer from reordering it and breaking the return-type contract.

claim: The default-OFF gate suppresses ALL toast kinds, including 'error'. Failure feedback that was previously always visible is now invisible out of the box: ExplorerPane 'Rename failed' / 'Delete failed' (src/renderer/components/ExplorerPane.tsx:39,46) and the op.ts generic failure path (src/renderer/op.ts:14) produce no UI at all for a fresh install or any user who hasn't opted in. A user whose action silently fails has no signal that anything went wrong.
evidence: pushToast short-circuits at `if (get().quick.toastsEnabled !== true) return id` before inspecting `kind`; grep confirms real error-kind call sites for file rename/delete and the op.ts catch-all. Toasts default OFF (REQ-005), so these errors are silent until the user finds and ticks Settings > General > 'Show toast notifications'.
recommendation: Let kind==='error' toasts bypass the enable gate (suppress only 'success'/'info' by default) so failures stay visible while routine confirmations are quiet; or, at minimum, make the checkbox copy explicit that errors are silenced too and confirm with the human that muting failure feedback is intended.
resolution: Resolved by iteration 1 error-bypass (see FINDING-DA-001): error-kind toasts now always render even when toastsEnabled is OFF; only success/info are suppressed.

claim: The native menu accelerator 'CmdOrCtrl+,' is hard-coded and duplicates the user-customizable `open-settings` keybinding (defaultChord Ctrl+,). Ctrl+, now has two independent owners and the menu accelerator is not driven by the keybinding registry. A registered menu accelerator intercepts the keystroke before the renderer keydown handler, so the menu — not the keybinding — effectively owns Ctrl+,. If a user remaps `open-settings` to another chord via KeybindingsSettings, the menu still opens Settings on Ctrl+,; if a user rebinds Ctrl+, to a different command, the menu accelerator shadows it and their customization silently never fires. The keybindings UI gives the false impression that Ctrl+,-for-settings is fully reconfigurable.
evidence: menu.ts adds `accelerator: 'CmdOrCtrl+,'` with no link to the keybinding registry; keybindings.ts:41 defines open-settings defaultChord c(true,false,','), dispatched at App.tsx:102 — both routes call openSettings({section:'general'}).
recommendation: Derive the menu accelerator from the keybinding registry, or omit it and register with `registerAccelerator: false` so the renderer keybinding stays the single source of truth and user remaps are honored. propose CONV: a native menu-item accelerator MUST be derived from (or kept in sync with) the user-customizable keybinding registry, never hard-coded to duplicate a rebindable command.

claim: The new top-level 'Edit' menu contains only 'Settings…' — no Undo/Redo/Cut/Copy/Paste/Select All. In an app that hosts a Monaco editor and terminals, users open an 'Edit' menu expecting clipboard/undo items and instead find a lone Settings entry; conversely, users hunting for Settings rarely look under 'Edit' (Settings/Preferences conventionally lives under File or a dedicated app menu on Windows). The label sets a misleading expectation and undercuts the discoverability the menu was meant to add.
evidence: menu.ts Edit submenu has exactly one item 'Settings…' and no clipboard/undo MenuItemConstructorOptions (no `role: 'cut'/'copy'/'paste'/'undo'`). The app ships Monaco + terminals where Edit-menu clipboard operations are an established platform convention.
recommendation: Either populate Edit with the standard roles (undo, redo, cut, copy, paste, selectAll) alongside Settings…, or relocate Settings… to a more conventional location (File, or a dedicated app/Preferences menu) so the menu name matches its contents. 'Edit' was a frozen brainstorm decision — surface for human confirmation rather than silently changing it.
resolution: Accepted by human decision in brainstorm (both iterations): the Edit menu holds Settings only for this feature; standard clipboard/undo Edit actions are explicitly deferred to a future feature. Not a defect — a scoped design choice.

claim: The toast checkbox label 'Show toast notifications' states WHAT it controls but conveys nothing about the new default-OFF state or the deliberate behavior change (toasts previously always rendered). A user who upgrades and finds their action feedback gone has no in-product explanation of why, nor that this single checkbox restores it; 'toast notifications' is itself opaque to a user who — because the default is off — has likely never seen one.
evidence: Checkbox renders `checked={toastsEnabled === true}` (default OFF per REQ-005) with bare label text and no helper/description; the only user-facing explanation of the default-off change lives in CHANGELOG.md (REQ-009), not the settings UI. Accessibility itself is fine: the <input> is wrapped by its <label> (implicit association, matching the copy-on-select / record-default sibling rows), so no htmlFor/aria-label is needed — this is a copy-clarity gap, not an a11y or layout inconsistency.
recommendation: Consider a short helper line under the checkbox (e.g. 'Off by default — pop-up confirmations and errors in the corner of the window') so the toggle and its new default are self-explanatory. Low priority; overlaps the discoverability concern in FINDING-UX-001.
resolution: Addressed in iteration 1: GeneralSettings checkbox label changed to 'Show success and info toast notifications (errors always show)', conveying the scope and that errors are never suppressed.

claim: Elevates the error-suppression concern (also raised as the doc/UX-side FINDING-UX-001, rated MEDIUM) to HIGH with a data-loss vector that the ux/doc framings missed: the EDITOR SAVE path uses a toast as the ONLY signal of a failed write. use-editor-tabs.ts:118/:134 call `runOp(() => api.fsWrite(...), pushToast, 'Save failed')`; on a write failure (read-only file, permission denied, disk full) runOp's error toast is now swallowed by the default-OFF gate, so the user presses Ctrl+S, sees nothing, and can quit believing the file was saved. This is silent data-loss-class failure, not merely a missing confirmation, and it directly breaks the documented runOp guarantee (docs/decisions.md:404-410: 'the user can only avoid a false success if the failure actually reaches it'). The spec's blanket REQ-006 gate ('no toast is added regardless of which call site invoked it') has no error carve-out; root cause is the brainstorm decision to gate unconditionally at pushToast (01-brainstorm.md:39-40). Filed as a distinct devils-advocate finding because the correct severity is HIGH and the editor-save data-loss path needs explicit human sign-off before this ships default-OFF.
evidence: src/renderer/editor/use-editor-tabs.ts:118 `if (!await runOp(() => api.fsWrite(path, content), useStore.getState().pushToast, 'Save failed')) return` (and :134 for the active buffer); src/renderer/op.ts:14 `toast(`${failMsg}: ...`, 'error')`; src/renderer/store/toasts-slice.ts:18 `if (get().quick.toastsEnabled !== true) return id` (no `kind` check); docs/decisions.md:404-410; 01-brainstorm.md:39-40.
recommendation: ESCALATE to a human. Resolve by making kind==='error' bypass the enable gate so failure feedback (especially Save failed) is never suppressed, while opt-in governs only success/info. propose CONV: a global notification-suppression preference MUST NOT suppress error/failure notifications; only informational/success notifications may be opt-in.
resolution: ESC-001 resolved by human (iteration 1): error toasts always render regardless of toastsEnabled. Implemented in src/renderer/store/toasts-slice.ts (`if (kind !== 'error' && quick.toastsEnabled !== true) return id`); pinned by TEST-019/TEST-020 (REQ-010). Editor Save-failed toast and all error feedback now surface; runOp guarantee preserved.

claim: REQ-003's second acceptance criterion is a MUST: 'subscribing then calling the returned unsubscribe stops further menu:open-settings callbacks from firing (CONV-002 boundary).' No test exercises this. TEST-003 only asserts CH.openSettings === 'menu:open-settings' (a channel-name constant). The test design waives the unsubscribe boundary as 'enforced by ... the App.tsx cleanup set in the launched-app e2e', but none of TEST-013..018 unmounts the subscription and re-sends the event to assert no callback fires. The REQ-003 unsubscribe contract is asserted by nobody; the gate's 'every REQ has a test' is satisfied by a constant-equality check that does not exercise the REQ's stated boundary — test theater for that acceptance bullet.
evidence: 02-spec.md REQ-003 Acceptance: 'subscribing then calling the returned unsubscribe stops further menu:open-settings callbacks from firing'; 04-tests.md TEST-003 asserts only the constant; 04-tests.md 'Not unit-tested' section concedes the unsubscribe is not directly tested and points at an e2e cleanup set that performs no unsubscribe-then-resend assertion.
recommendation: Add a headless test of the subscription wiring (inject a fake emitter into pushChannel) that subscribes, unsubscribes, re-emits, and asserts the callback did not fire; or an e2e that detaches and re-sends. Do not claim REQ-003's unsubscribe acceptance is met by the constant check alone.

claim: The live 'runOp' decision in docs/decisions.md documents a guarantee that fallible user actions (editor save, env-vault add/create) surface their failure to the user via a toast ('the user can only avoid a false success if the failure actually reaches it'). REQ-005/006 make all toasts default-OFF at the single pushToast chokepoint with no carve-out for error toasts, so on a fresh/legacy install those documented failure toasts are silently suppressed. The doc now actively contradicts shipped default behavior, and neither REQ-009 nor TASK-011 lists decisions.md for reconciliation. The underlying concern (data-loss failure toasts swallowed by default) is the same root issue as FINDING-DA-001/FINDING-UX-001.
evidence: docs/decisions.md:404-410 documents the toast-as-only-failure-signal guarantee; src/renderer/store/toasts-slice.ts:19 gates all kinds; REQ-009/TASK-011 do not name decisions.md.
recommendation: Resolve the underlying error-suppression decision (see FINDING-DA-001); then have doc-sync reconcile docs/decisions.md to whatever is decided. If errors bypass the gate, the runOp guarantee holds and only needs a note; if not, decisions.md must be amended.
note: Re-added after a concurrent-write clobber lost the doc-drift entries from findings.json during parallel review.
resolution: Resolved by the iteration-1 error carve-out: error toasts always surface, so the docs/decisions.md runOp failure-feedback guarantee holds and needs no amendment. doc-sync (phase 7) added the confirming note under the runOp decision in docs/decisions.md (the toast preference governs success/info only; error toasts always render; the guarantee is preserved).

claim: TASK-011 named CLAUDE.md as a doc target, but the diff leaves it untouched. src/main/menu.ts, the menu:open-settings channel, the moved Settings entry, and the toastsEnabled QuickStore field are undocumented there. Omission, not contradiction (CLAUDE.md never listed the gear or enumerated channels), so it is a doc-sync TODO.
evidence: git diff shows no CLAUDE.md change; menu.ts/menu:open-settings/toastsEnabled are new surfaces not reflected in the where-things-live table.
recommendation: doc-sync (phase 7) updates CLAUDE.md's where-things-live table and any channel references to include the Edit menu and the toast preference.
note: Re-added after a concurrent-write clobber lost the doc-drift entries from findings.json during parallel review.
resolution: doc-sync (phase 7) added two CLAUDE.md where-things-live rows ('Native app menu / Settings entry' -> src/main/menu.ts + menu:open-settings, and 'Toast notifications' -> toasts-slice.ts/toastsEnabled). The new Edit-menu Settings entry, the channel, and the toastsEnabled preference are now documented.
